title: Complete Modern History – GV Sir Headshot Series
source: GvKaksha / Eduquity Pattern – SSC CGL/CHSL/MTS/Delhi Police 2026

table.topic: British Expansion in India (Carnatic Wars, Plassey, Buxar)
table.sub_areas: Carnatic Wars, Nawabs of Bengal, Battle of Plassey, Battle of Buxar, Treaty of Allahabad
Shivaji, Ashta Pradhan, Peshwas, Anglo-Maratha Wars, Battle of Panipat III
Guru Nanak, Guru Gobind Singh, Maharaja Ranjit Singh, Anglo-Sikh Wars
Robert Clive, Warren Hastings, Cornwallis, Wellesley, Bentick, Dalhousie, Curzon, Irwin, Mountbatten

table.topic: Timeline – Liberal to Extremist Phase, Gandhi Era, Civil Disobedience, WW-II Impact
table.sub_areas: INC formation, Surat split, Non-Cooperation, Civil Disobedience, Quit India
Bhagat Singh, Chandrashekhar Azad, Subhash Chandra Bose, INA, Lal Bal Pal

table.topic: Socio-Cultural Reform Movements and Their Leaders
table.sub_areas: Brahmo Samaj, Arya Samaj, Aligarh Movement, Ramakrishna Mission
Santhal Hool, Munda Ulgulan, Kol Rebellion, Birsa Munda
Jantar Mantar observatories, Jaipur founding, Governor of Malwa and Agra

chapters.chapter: British Expansion in India – Carnatic Wars

subtopics.name: First Carnatic War (1746–1748)
content: Cause: Extension of War of Austrian Succession in Europe; Britain and France on opposing sides
Major Event: French army captured Madras
Battle of St. Thome (Adyar) 1746: French force led by Captain Paradise defeated Nawab Anwaruddin's larger army #imp
Conclusion: Treaty of Aix-la-Chapelle (1748) – Madras returned to British; French received North American territories

subtopics.name: Second Carnatic War (1749–1754)
content: Linked to succession disputes in Carnatic and Hyderabad
British supported Muhammad Ali; French supported Chanda Sahib
British officer Robert Clive attacked Arcot (1751) #imp
Ended with Treaty of Pondicherry (1754); British position strengthened

subtopics.name: Third Carnatic War (1756–1763)
content: Indian counterpart of the Seven Years' War
Decisive battle: Battle of Wandiwash (1760) – British commander Eyre Coote defeated French commander Count de Lally #imp
Ended with Treaty of Paris (1763)
France retained only trading posts; political power extinguished

subtopics.name: Battle of Plassey & Buxar
content: Alivardi Khan (1740–1756) – Nawab of Bengal; grandson Siraj-ud-Daulah succeeded him #imp
1756: Siraj-ud-Daulah attacked Fort William (Calcutta); Black Hole of Calcutta incident (British propaganda)
1757 – Battle of Plassey (June 23): Robert Clive vs Siraj-ud-Daulah; British won due to Mir Jafar's betrayal #imp
After Plassey: Mir Jafar became Nawab; real power shifted to British
1760: Mir Jafar removed; Mir Qasim became Nawab; opposed British trade concessions
Battle of Buxar (October 22, 1764): British vs Mir Qasim + Shuja-ud-Daulah (Awadh) + Shah Alam II (Mughal Emperor) #imp
British army led by Hector Munro; British won
Treaty of Allahabad (1765): Company received Diwani of Bengal, Bihar, Odisha from Shah Alam II; Awadh made buffer state

subtopics.name: PYQs – British Expansion
content: PYQ: Who became Nawab of Bengal after Alivardi Khan = Sirajuddaulah (Ans: D – SSC CHSL)
PYQ: Shujauddaulah was Nawab of = Awadh (Ans: B – SSC CHSL)
PYQ: British EIC's first major victory in India = Battle of Plassey (Ans: D – SSC CHSL)
PYQ: Battle of Plassey fought in = 1757 (Ans: A – SSC CGL)
PYQ: Battle of Buxar took place in = 1764 (Ans: B – SSC CHSL)
PYQ: Treaty of Paris (1763) restricted French to trading posts only + French could not fortify factories = A&R both true, R explains A (Ans: B – Steno Exam)

subtopics.name: Overview
content: Wodeyar dynasty established in 1399 by Yaduraya Wodeyar
Key rulers: Hyder Ali and Tipu Sultan
Final battle: Srirangapatnam (1799)

table.parties: Hyder Ali/Tipu Sultan vs British (Warren Hastings triggered it by attacking French port of Mahe)
table.outcome: Hyder Ali died 1782 (tumor); Porto Novo defeat; Tipu succeeded

table.parties: Tipu Sultan vs British EIC + Marathas + Nizam; initiated by Lord Cornwallis
table.outcome: Tipu Sultan defeated; ceded half his kingdom

table.parties: Tipu Sultan vs Lord Wellesley; Tipu refused to break French ties or accept Subsidiary Alliance
table.outcome: Tipu Sultan (Tiger of Mysore) died defending Srirangapatnam
table.treaty: Mysore restored to Wodeyars under Krishnaraja III who accepted Subsidiary Alliance

subtopics.name: PYQs – Anglo-Mysore Wars
content: PYQ: Tipu Sultan became ruler of Mysore in = 1782 (Ans: A – SSC CHSL)
PYQ: Capital of Tipu Sultan's kingdom = Seringapatam/Srirangapatnam (Ans: B – SSC CHSL)
PYQ: Who established close relations with French & modernized army = Tipu Sultan (Ans: C – SSC CHSL)
PYQ: Third Anglo-Mysore War fought in = 1790–92 (Ans: A – SSC CHSL)
PYQ: Combined forces that attacked Tipu Sultan in 1792 = Marathas + Nizam + East India Company (Ans: D – SSC CHSL)

subtopics.name: Key Facts
content: Full name: Jai Singh II; Kachwaha Rajput ruler of Amer (Jaipur)
Born: 1688; title 'Sawai' (one and a quarter times superior) given by Aurangzeb #imp
Governor of Malwa multiple times between 1714 and 1737
In 1722, received governorship of Agra; made Jaipur his capital
Shifted capital from Amer to Jaipur in 1727 #imp
Built five Jantar Mantar observatories: Delhi, Jaipur, Ujjain, Mathura, Varanasi #imp

subtopics.name: PYQs – Sawai Raja Jai Singh
content: PYQ: Around 1713, Sawai Raja Jai Singh appointed governor of = Malwa (Ans: D – SSC CHSL)
PYQ: In 1722, Sawai Raja Jai Singh given governorship of = Agra (Ans: C – SSC CHSL)
PYQ: New capital founded by Sawai Raja Jai Singh of Amber = Jaipur (Ans: A – SSC CHSL)
PYQ: Astronomical observatories constructed by Sawai Jai Singh = Five (Ans: A – SSC CHSL)

subtopics.name: Shivaji Maharaj
content: Born: 1630; Founder of Maratha Empire
Father: Shahaji Bhonsle (Bijapur Adilshahi officer); Mother: Jijabai
Religious Guru: Samarth Ramdas; Political Guru: Dadaji Konddev
Capital: Raigad
Military strategy: Guerrilla warfare known as 'Ganimi Kava' or 'Shiva Sutra' #imp
1659: Killed Afzal Khan (Bijapur commander) in Battle of Pratapgarh
1665: Treaty of Purandar with Raja Jai Singh I (Aurangzeb's representative)
Declared Chhatrapati in 1674; founded Maratha Empire
Died: April 3, 1680; succeeded by son Sambhaji
Sambhaji defeated Mughal forces in Battle of Wai (1687)
Sambhaji captured, tortured & executed by Aurangzeb in 1689

subtopics.name: Peshwas and Maratha Confederacy
content: Bajirao I (Thorle), son of Balaji Vishwanath – fought 40+ battles
Maratha Confederacy: Scindias (Ranoji Shinde – Gwalior), Holkars (Malharrao – Indore), Gaekwads (Pilaji – Baroda), Pawars (Udaiji – Dhar)
Balaji Bajirao (Nanasaheb I), son of Bajirao I – captured Attock and Peshawar (1758); defeated Alivardi Khan of Bengal (1751)
Third Battle of Panipat (1761): Ahmad Shah Abdali defeated Marathas led by Sadashivrao Bhau (nephew of Peshwa Bajirao I) #imp

subtopics.name: Anglo-Maratha Wars
content: First Anglo-Maratha War (1775–1782): Cause = power struggle between Madhavrao & Raghunathrao; Treaty of Surat (1775) rejected by Calcutta Council; ended with Treaty of Salbai (1782) – agreed by Warren Hastings & Mahadji Shinde #imp
Second Anglo-Maratha War (1803–1805): Peshwa Bajirao II became Peshwa after Madhavrao Narayan suicide (1795); Treaty of Bassein (1802) brought Peshwa under British control #imp
Third Anglo-Maratha War (1817–1819): Marathas wanted to reclaim lost territory; sheltered Pindaris; Treaty of Poona (1818) – Peshwa Bajirao II retired on pension; Treaty of Nagpur (1816) with Bhonsle

subtopics.name: PYQs – Maratha Empire
content: PYQ: Which Maratha ruler established Empire in 1674 = Shivaji (Ans: C – Steno Exam)
PYQ: Backbone of Maratha army = Kunbis (Ans: C – SSC CHSL)
PYQ: Military strategy Shivaji used = Guerrilla warfare (Ans: C)
PYQ: Shivaji's successor = Chhatrapati Sambhaji (Ans: C – SSC CHSL)
PYQ: Principal minister in Maratha kingdom = Peshwa (Ans: B – SSC CHSL)
PYQ: First Anglo-Maratha War ended with = Treaty of Salbai (Ans: C)
PYQ: Baji Rao I popularly referred to as = Baji Rao Ballal (Ans: C)
PYQ: Father of Baji Rao I = Peshwa Balaji Vishwanath (Ans: B – SSC CHSL)
PYQ: Maratha campaigns in Rajasthan put pressure from = 1740s (Ans: D – SSC CHSL)
PYQ: Shivaji's exploits against which forces = Both Mughal & Bijapur (Ans: C – SSC CHSL)

table.key_facts: Founder of Sikhism; born in Rai Bhoi ki Talwandi (Nankana Sahib); introduced Langar system & twin institutions of Sangat and Pangat; contemporary of Babur; died at Kartarpur
Compiled Adi Granth; completed Amritsar; founded Tarn Taran Sahib; first Sikh martyr – executed on orders of Jahangir for supporting Prince Khusrau #imp
Founded Anandpur; assassinated by Aurangzeb in Delhi 1675; second martyr in Sikh history
Born Dec 22, 1666 in Patna; 10th and last Guru; founded Khalsa on 13 April 1699 (Vaisakhi); introduced 5 Ks; declared Guru Granth Sahib as eternal Guru; died October 7, 1708 #imp

subtopics.name: Maharaja Ranjit Singh & Anglo-Sikh Wars
content: Born: November 13, 1780 in Gujranwala (Pakistan); died 1839 AD
Title: Sher-e-Punjab; Capital: Lahore
Founder of Sikh Empire; captured Lahore in 1799; established secular Sikh state
Assumed title of Maharaja in 1801 AD
Treaty of Amritsar (1809): Between British EIC (Charles T. Metcalfe) & Ranjit Singh; Sutlej River fixed as boundary #imp
First Anglo-Sikh War (1845–46): British won; Sikh army crossed Sutlej on Dec 11, 1845 violating Treaty of Amritsar
Second Anglo-Sikh War (1848–49): British won; causes = humiliation of Queen Jindan; exile to Banaras; murder of British officers by Mulraj (Governor of Multan); Sikh surrendered at Rawalpindi 1849
First Anglo-Afghan War (1838–42): Afghans under Amir Dost Mohammad defeated British; Lord Lawrence followed Masterly Inactivity (1864–1869)
Second Anglo-Afghan War (1878–80): Amir Sher Ali stopped British envoy at Khyber Pass; Lord Lytton used Forward Policy; Yaqub Khan signed Treaty of Gandamak (1879)
Third Anglo-Afghan War (1919): Ended with Treaty of Rawalpindi; Afghanistan got independence on 19 August 1919 under Emir Amanullah Khan

subtopics.name: PYQs – Sikh Gurus and Empire
content: PYQ: Which Sikh Guru instituted Khalsa in 1699 = Guru Gobind Singh (Ans: D – SSC CHSL)
PYQ: In which year Guru Gobind Singh died = 1708 (Ans: A – SSC CHSL)
PYQ: Combined army of Sikh Jathas and Misls in 18th century = Dal Khalsa (Ans: D – SSC CHSL)
PYQ: Maharaja Ranjit Singh established capital at Lahore in = 1799 (Ans: D – SSC CHSL)

subtopics.name: Robert Clive (1757–1760 / 1765–1767)
content: Considered founder of British rule in India; first British Governor of Bengal
Defeated Siraj-ud-Daulah in Battle of Plassey (June 23, 1757)
Signed Treaty of Allahabad (1765) with Shah Alam II and Nawab of Awadh
Introduced Dual Government system in 1765 (lasted until 1772)
Committed suicide on November 22, 1774 in London

subtopics.name: Warren Hastings (1774–1785) – First Governor-General of Bengal
content: Regulating Act 1773: Governor of Bengal made first Governor-General of India; Warren Hastings was first GG
Abolished Dual System Administration & system of Dastaks
Supreme Court established in Calcutta in 1774; first judge: Elijah Impey
Established civil (revenue/diwani) and criminal courts
Assisted founding of Asiatic Society (1784) by Sir William Jones
Fought First Anglo-Maratha War (1775–82) and Second Anglo-Mysore War (1780–84)

subtopics.name: Lord Cornwallis (1786–1793)
content: Introduced Zamindari System (Permanent Settlement) in 1793 in Bengal #imp
Father of Civil Services; Father of Judicial Reforms in India (Cornwallis Code 1793)
Established Police Thanas for law and order
Founded Sanskrit College at Benaras (Jonathan Duncan) and Mint at Calcutta
Fought Third Anglo-Mysore War (1790–92)

subtopics.name: Richard Wellesley (1798–1805)
content: Called himself 'The Lion of Bengal'
Started Subsidiary Alliance system; 1st alliance with Nizam of Hyderabad (1798) #imp
States under Subsidiary Alliance: Hyderabad–1798, Mysore–1799, Tanjore–1799, Awadh–1801, Peshwa–1802, Bhonsle (Nagpur)–1803, Scindia (Gwalior)–1804, Holkar (Indore)–1818
Treaty of Bassein (1802): Between Maratha Peshwa Bajirao II and British
Established Fort William College Calcutta (1800) for training civil servants
Fought 4th Anglo-Mysore War (Tipu Sultan's death) & 2nd Anglo-Maratha War

subtopics.name: Lord Hastings (1813–1823)
content: Anglo-Nepalese War 1814–1816; Treaty of Sagauli (1815, ratified 1816)
Third Anglo-Maratha War (1817–19)
British paramountcy fully established during his tenure

subtopics.name: Lord William Bentick (1828–1835)
content: 1828–1833: Last Governor-General of Bengal
1833–1835: First Governor-General of India
Abolished Sati Pratha & other cruel rites in 1829 #imp
Suppressed Kol Rebellion of Chhota Nagpur tribes (1831)
Charter Act of 1833 passed in his tenure
Reformed Mahalwari System in 1833; Formation of 1st Law Commission in 1834
Raja Ram Mohan Roy founded Brahmo Samaj at Calcutta in 1828

subtopics.name: Lord Dalhousie (1848–1856)
content: Introduced Doctrine of Lapse: Satara (1848), Jhansi (1853), Nagpur (1854) annexed
Started Railways (1853: Bombay to Thane, first train), Telegraph (1851), Post Office
Wood's Education Despatch (1854) – foundation of modern education
Annexation of Punjab (1849) after 2nd Anglo-Sikh War
Annexation of Awadh (1856) – on grounds of misgovernance

subtopics.name: Lord Canning (1856–1862) – First Viceroy of India
content: Revolt of 1857 occurred during his tenure
Government of India Act (1858) – Company rule replaced by Crown rule; Canning became first Viceroy
Indian Councils Act (1861) introduced

subtopics.name: Lord Lytton (1876–1880)
content: Royal Titles Act 1876: Queen Victoria assumed title 'Empress of India' (Kaiser-e-Hind) #imp
Arms Act (1878): Mandatory licence for Indians to possess arms
Proposed Statutory Civil Service (1878–79); lowered max age from 21 to 19 years
Second Anglo-Afghan War (1878–80); followed Forward Policy

subtopics.name: Lord Ripon (1880–1884)
content: First synchronous Official Census in 1881
Repeal of Vernacular Press Act (1882)
Establishment of Punjab University (1882)
Government resolution on local self-government (1882); Father of Local Self-Government
Education Reforms – Hunter Commission (1882)
Ilbert Bill Controversy: Grant Indian magistrates authority to try European/British citizens

subtopics.name: Lord Curzon (1899–1905)
content: Indian Coinage & Paper Currency Act (1899) – India on Gold Standard
Munda (Ulgulan) Rebellion led by Birsa Munda (1899–1900)
Calcutta Corporation Act of 1899
In 1905: Bengal divided into East Bengal and West Bengal #imp
Swadeshi Movement (1905–1911) against Partition of Bengal by Lal Bal Pal and Aurbindo Ghosh

subtopics.name: Lord Minto-II (1905–1910)
content: Jamsetji Tata established TISCO (1907)
Muslim League established by Aga Khan III & Khwaja Salimullah (Nawab of Dhaka) in 1906 #imp
Foundation stone of Victoria Memorial laid (1906)
Split in Congress (1907) at Surat session
Indian Council Act 1909 (Morley–Minto Reform): Introduced Communal Electorate #imp

subtopics.name: Lord Hardinge-II (1910–1916)
content: Annulment of Partition of Bengal by King George V (1911) #imp
Transfer of capital from Calcutta to Delhi (1911) #imp
Coronation Durbar of King George V – Delhi Durbar III (1911)
Partition of Bengal to form Bihar and Orissa province (1912)
World War I (1914–1918); McMahon border line between India and China (1914)
Mahatma Gandhi returned to India from South Africa (1915)

subtopics.name: Lord Chelmsford (1916–1921)
content: Indian Home Rule Movement by Bal Gangadhar Tilak and Annie Besant (1916)
Lucknow Pact (1916) between INC and Muslim League
Jallianwala Bagh massacre (1919) – General O'Dwyer #imp
Champaran Satyagraha (1917) – Gandhi's first Satyagraha in India

subtopics.name: Lord Irwin (1926–1931)
content: Death of Lala Lajpat Rai (1928)
All India Youth Congress in 1928
Dandi March (March 12, 1930) by Gandhi – Civil Disobedience Movement launched #imp
Gandhi–Irwin Pact (1931)

subtopics.name: Key Facts
content: Also called 'First War of Indian Independence' / Sepoy Mutiny / Great Revolt
Immediate cause: Introduction of Enfield rifle cartridges greased with cow & pig fat (offensive to Hindus and Muslims)
First spark: Mangal Pandey at Barrackpore (March 29, 1857)
Official start: May 10, 1857 at Meerut
Viceroy: Lord Canning; Ended 1858 – Company rule replaced by Crown
Government of India Act 1858: Secretary of State for India and Council of India established

table.leader: Nana Saheb (Peshwa Bajirao II's adopted son)

subtopics.name: Early Phase – Moderate and Extremist
content: Indian National Congress (INC) founded: 1885 by A.O. Hume; first session in Bombay, presided by W.C. Bonnerjee
Moderate Phase (1885–1905): Leaders – Gopal Krishna Gokhale, Dadabhai Naoroji, Pherozeshah Mehta; method: petitions and prayers
Extremist Phase (1905–1920): Leaders – Bal Gangadhar Tilak, Lala Lajpat Rai, Bipin Chandra Pal (Lal Bal Pal)
Surat Split (1907): Congress divided into Moderates (Gokhale) and Extremists (Tilak) #imp
Morley-Minto Reforms (1909): Communal electorate introduced; divisive policy

subtopics.name: Gandhi Era – Non-Cooperation to Civil Disobedience
content: Gandhi returned to India from South Africa (1915)
Champaran Satyagraha (1917): Against indigo plantation oppression; Gandhi's first Satyagraha in India
Non-Cooperation Movement (1920–22): Gandhi; boycott of foreign goods, courts, schools
Chauri Chaura incident (Feb 5, 1922): Violence led Gandhi to suspend NCM
Simon Commission (1927): All British members; boycotted with 'Simon Go Back'
Dandi March (March 12, 1930): Gandhi marched 241 miles to make salt; launched Civil Disobedience Movement #imp
Gandhi–Irwin Pact (1931): CDM suspended
Round Table Conferences: 1930 (INC absent), 1931 (Gandhi attended), 1932
Poona Pact (1932): Between Gandhi and B.R. Ambedkar on separate electorate for depressed classes

subtopics.name: Quit India Movement & Independence
content: Quit India Movement (August 8, 1942): 'Do or Die'; launched at Bombay session of INC; Viceroy Lord Linlithgow #imp
INA (Indian National Army) formed by Subhash Chandra Bose (Netaji); slogan 'Dilli Chalo'
Cabinet Mission (1946): Proposed Union of India with provinces and princely states
Indian Independence Act (July 18, 1947): India and Pakistan as two dominions
India got independence: August 15, 1947; Mountbatten was last Viceroy

table.name: Mahatma Gandhi
table.nickname_title: Father of the Nation / Bapu / Mahatma
table.key_contribution: Non-Cooperation, CDM, Quit India; Dandi March; Champaran Satyagraha

table.name: Subhash Chandra Bose
table.key_contribution: Founded INA; 'Dilli Chalo' slogan; 'Give me blood, I will give you freedom'

table.name: Bhagat Singh
table.key_contribution: Killed Saunders; bombed Central Assembly (1929); hanged March 23, 1931 with Rajguru & Sukhdev

table.name: Chandrashekhar Azad
table.key_contribution: HSRA (Hindustan Socialist Republican Association); died in Alfred Park, Allahabad (1931)

table.name: Bal Gangadhar Tilak
table.key_contribution: Extremist leader; 'Swaraj is my birthright'; started Ganesh Festival & Shivaji Festival

table.name: Lala Lajpat Rai
table.key_contribution: Died from injuries after police lathi charge during Simon Commission protest (1928)

table.name: Bipin Chandra Pal
table.key_contribution: Part of Lal Bal Pal trio; advocated complete independence

table.name: Dadabhai Naoroji
table.key_contribution: Drain Theory – exposed British economic exploitation

table.name: Gopal Krishna Gokhale
table.key_contribution: Moderate leader; founder of Servants of India Society (1905)

table.name: Annie Besant
table.key_contribution: Home Rule Movement (1916); founded Theosophical Society India

subtopics.name: Important Revolutionary Organizations
content: Abhinav Bharat Society: Founded by V.D. Savarkar (1904)
Anushilan Samiti: Bengal revolutionary organization
Ghadar Party (1913): Founded by Lala Hardayal in San Francisco
HSRA (Hindustan Socialist Republican Association): Bhagat Singh, Chandrashekhar Azad
INA (Indian National Army): Founded by Rash Behari Bose (1942); Netaji Subhash Chandra Bose took command
Chapekar Brothers (Damodar, Balkrishna, Vasudeo): Assassinated a British official during plague epidemic in Pune

table.key_facts: Monotheistic; opposed idol worship, sati, caste; 'Father of Indian Renaissance'
Back to Vedas; opposed idol worship; started Shuddhi movement; Din-i-Ilahi opposed
Vedanta philosophy; social service; Chicago speech (1893) – World Parliament of Religions
Founded MAO College (later Aligarh Muslim University); modernize Muslim education
Social reform among Maharashtrian Hindus; opposed caste discrimination
Universal brotherhood; Annie Besant became president

table.cause: Alienation of tribal land by outsiders; suppressed by Lord William Bentick
Exploitation by moneylenders, zamindars, and British officials
Against exploitation by landlords and Christian missionaries; wanted Munda raj (self-rule)
Against British restrictions on forest use and the Arms Act
Demanded two-thirds share of crop for sharecroppers (instead of half)

pyqs.question: Sweeping geopolitical shifts from defeat of Nawab of Carnatic's army = Exposed systemic weaknesses; EIC shifted from trade to political hegemony
Treaty of Paris (1763) restricted French to trading posts + French could not fortify factories = Both A&R true, R explains A
Who became Nawab of Bengal after Alivardi Khan = Sirajuddaulah
Shujauddaulah was Nawab of which region = Awadh
EIC's first major victory in India = Battle of Plassey
Battle of Plassey fought in = 1757
Battle of Buxar took place in = 1764
Tipu Sultan became ruler of Mysore in = 1782
Capital of Tipu Sultan's kingdom = Seringapatam (Srirangapatnam)
Who established close relations with French and modernized army = Tipu Sultan
Third Anglo-Mysore War fought in = 1790–92
Combined forces that attacked Tipu Sultan in 1792 = Marathas + Nizam + EIC
Around 1713, Sawai Raja Jai Singh appointed governor of = Malwa
In 1722, Sawai Raja Jai Singh given governorship of = Agra
New capital founded by Sawai Raja Jai Singh of Amber = Jaipur
Astronomical observatories constructed by Sawai Jai Singh = Five
Maratha ruler who established Empire in 1674 by declaring Chhatrapati = Shivaji
Backbone of Maratha army = Kunbis
Military strategy Shivaji used = Guerrilla warfare (Ganimi Kava)
Successor of Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj = Chhatrapati Sambhaji
Principal minister in Maratha kingdom = Peshwa
First Anglo-Maratha War ended with = Treaty of Salbai
Baji Rao I popularly referred to as = Baji Rao Ballal
Father of Baji Rao I = Peshwa Balaji Vishwanath
Maratha campaigns in Rajasthan put pressure from = 1740s decade
Shivaji's exploits against which forces made him legendary = Both Mughal & Bijapur
Which Sikh Guru instituted Khalsa in 1699 = Guru Gobind Singh
In which year Guru Gobind Singh died = 1708
Combined army of Sikh Jathas and Misls in 18th century = Dal Khalsa
Maharaja Ranjit Singh established capital at Lahore in = 1799
Governor-General of British India from 1773 to 1785 = Warren Hastings
In which year EIC introduced Permanent Settlement = 1793
Primary objective of Ring-Fencing Policy by British = Create buffer states to defend British territories
Primary consequence for Indian states entering Subsidiary Alliance = Lost independent foreign policy and military capacity (under British political control)
Subsidiary Alliance condition = Allied rulers had to keep a British Resident in their court
Year Nawab of Awadh was forced to give half his territory to EIC = 1801
Under which GG was policy of 'paramountcy' initiated = Lord Hastings
Indian Arms Act passed by British Government in = 1878
Ilbert Bill controversy of 1883–84 related to = Allowing Indian judges to try European offenders in criminal cases
Which bill provided trial of British persons by Indians = Ilbert Bill of 1883
British colonial architecture adopted lime mortar + design methods enabled arches, domes = Both correct but Stmt 2 does not explain Stmt 1
British capital in 18th century reinvested in industrial development + capital controlled by merchants/industrialists = A false, R true
Chapekar Brothers assassinated British official during plague epidemic in which city = Pune
Bombay and Madras government response to 1839 Draft Act on slavery = Bombay saw no need for law; Madras questioned expediency
Robert Clive appointed Governor of Bengal around = 1764 (1765)
Tragic incident in Robert Clive's life in 1774 = Committed suicide
Responsibilities of Madras and Bombay governors after 1773 = Had to report to Governor-General on war, peace, and revenue
